recalls.Description: FUNTOK is recalling its 24V 2-Seater Ride-On Trucks, model DLS-K03, due to potential fire and burn hazards. These trucks, available in red, black, white, and pink, feature '4x4' on the tailgate and 'TURBO DIESEL' on the side. The brand name and model number can be found on the User Manual cover and the packaging.
recalls.Title: FUNTOK 24V Ride-On Trucks Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Shenzhen Luobei Trading Co. at 800-249-9581, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, email service@funtok.net or visit https://funtok.net/pages/voluntary-product-safety-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled FUNTOK 24V 2-Seater Ride-On Truck (Side view)
Recalled FUNTOK 24V 2-Seater Ride-On Truck (Front view)
Recalled FUNTOK 24V 2-Seater Ride-On Truck (Rear view)
Recalled FUNTOK 24V 2-Seater Ride-On Truck

Injuries.Name: Shenzhen Luobei Trading Co. has received 11 reports of the ride-on trucks catching fire, sparking, burning, melting, overheating and smoking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and TikTok.com from October 2025 to December 2025 for approximately $240

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Luobei Trading Co., Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Overheating circuit board leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the truck, unplug if charging, and contact for a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: FUNTOK recalls 24V Ride-On Trucks due to fire hazards. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

recalls.Description: Gagaku-branded ice cream shop toy playsets are being recalled due to serious safety concerns. These sets consist of 16 wooden components, including a cash register, three ice cream cones, six scoops of ice cream, four popsicles, two cupcakes, a plastic credit card, five plastic dollar bills, a menu, and a two-tier pink display box marked "ICE-CREAM". The product is identifiable by the label on the underside that reads "GAGAKU" and "Model: KABI-0176 G062401IC".
recalls.Title: Gagaku Ice Cream Toy Playsets Recalled Due to Magnet Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Brandworl.USA via email at brandworl@gmail.com.

Products.Name: Gagaku Ice Cream Shop Toy Playsets

Images.Caption: Recalled Gagaku Wooden Ice Cream Shop Toy Playsets
"GAGAKU" and "Model: KABI-0176 G062401IC" are printed on a label on the underside of the toy

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from October 2024 through June 2025, priced between $17 and $30.

Distributors.Name: Brandworl Management Co., Limited, dba Brandworl.USA, of Hong Kong

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion from high-powered magnets that can detach and pose serious health risks.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Brandworl.USA for a full refund. Destroy the toy by marking each piece with "RECALLED" and send a photo of the destroyed toy to Brandworl@gmail.com before disposing of it.

recalls.metaDescription: Gagaku ice cream toy playsets recalled due to magnet ingestion risk. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Backyard Kids is recalling the KidKraft Farm to Table play kitchen, designed for children aged 3 and older. This product features a black and light gray design with wood-like countertops, measuring approximately 44 inches in length, 13 ¾ inches in width, and 43 inches in height. The model number 53411 can be found on a sticker located on the back of the kitchen.
recalls.Title: Backyard Kids Recalls KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchens Due to Strangulation Risk; One Fatal Incident Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Backyard Kids toll-free at 800-882-0234, Monday through Friday from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at recall@kidkraft.com. Additional details are available online at www.kidkraft.com/pages/recall.

Products.Name: KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchen
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 192,000 (In addition, 12 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchen
Recalled KidKraft Farm to Table Play Kitchen (the hooks are circled)

Injuries.Name: In February 2023 in Oregon, a 23-month-old child was asphyxiated after his shirt got caught on one of the hooks while climbing and crawling through the opening at the back of the play kitchen where the toy kitchen accessories are held. The child was unable to rescue himself and died from strangulation and asphyxia.

Retailers.Name: These play kitchens were available for purchase online at KidKraft.com, Amazon.com, and Walmart.com from 2018 until July 2025, priced between $120 and $270.

Distributors.Name: Backyard Kids, LLC, of Plano, Texas

Hazards.Name: Climbing or crawling children may get their clothing caught on hooks designed for toy kitchen accessories, leading to a strangulation hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the play kitchens immediately, remove the original hooks, and contact Backyard Kids for free replacement hooks.

recalls.metaDescription: Backyard Kids recalls KidKraft Farm to Table play kitchens due to a strangulation hazard. Learn how to get free replacement hooks.

recalls.Description: Red Toolbox has announced a recall of the Stanley Jr. Kids Wheelbarrow and 7-piece Garden Set, specifically the long hoe and rake, due to excessive lead levels that violate federal safety standards. The affected items feature a yellow-painted wooden handle and black-painted metal components. The wheelbarrow is identifiable by a white label on its bottom indicating 'Red Toolbox' and a manufacturing date of December 2023. Only the long hoe and rake are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Red Toolbox Issues Recall for Stanley Jr. Kids Garden Sets Due to Lead Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Red Toolbox via email at CS@recall-rtb.com or visit their recall website at https://www.recall-rtb.com/ for further details on obtaining a free replacement.

Products.Name: Stanley Jr. Kids Wheelbarrow and 7-piece Garden Set

Images.Caption: Recalled Long Hoe and Long Rake
Stanley Jr. 7-Piece Kids Wheelbarrow and 7-piece Garden Set Containing Recalled Long Hoe and Rake
Tracking Label on Bottom of Wheelbarrow

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at Costco stores and online at www.costco.com from February 2024 to June 2024 for approximately $35.

Distributors.Name: Red Toolbox USA, of El Segundo, California

Hazards.Name: Lead poisoning risk due to excessive lead levels in painted long hoe and rake

Remedies.Name: Stop using the long hoe and rake immediately and visit the recall website for instructions on obtaining a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Red Toolbox recalls Stanley Jr. Kids Garden Sets due to lead poisoning hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement.

recalls.Description:
MGA Entertainment is recalling its Miniverse Make It Mini Sets that contain unused liquid resins due to safety concerns. These sets include various models such as 'Make It Mini Appliances,' 'Make It Mini Food,' and 'Make It Mini Lifestyle.' The liquid resins can cause irritation or sensitization when inhaled, touched, or ingested, posing a risk to both children and adults. The affected products are packaged in spheres or boxes with distinct labeling.

The resin packaging may resemble food items, and while the resins become safe after curing, they contain acrylates that exceed the allowable limits in children's products as per the Federal Hazardous Substances Act. Consumers are urged to stop using these products immediately and follow the recall instructions to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: MGA Entertainment Issues Recall for Miniverse Make It Mini Sets Due to Potential Skin, Eye, and Respiratory Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact MGA Entertainment at 800-222-4685 between 9 a.m. and 8 p.m. ET on weekdays and 9 a.m. to 2 p.m. ET on Saturdays. You can also email mvcustomer_care@mgae.com or visit https://www.mgae.com/customer-care/recalls for further details.

Products.Name: Miniverse Make It Mini Sets with Liquid Resins
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 21 million (In addition, about 1 million in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – Food
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – Appliances
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini - Lifestyle
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – Kitchen
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini – All You Can Eat
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini Appliances
Recalled Miniverse Make It Mini Lifestyle
Recalled Make It Mini Food – 18 Pack
Recalled Make It Mini Food Ice Cream Social - Front
Recalled Make It Mini Food Ice Cream Social - Opened

Injuries.Name: MGA has received 26 reports of incidents with this product from children and adults, including reports of skin burns and irritation, and respiratory irritation, with one report that a consumer's asthma was triggered.

Retailers.Name: Available at Target, Walmart, Family Dollar, Dollar General, ALDI, Hobby Lobby, and online at Amazon.com, shop.mgae.com, Target.com, and Walmart.com from October 2022 through June 2024.

Hazards.Name: Potential skin, eye, and respiratory irritation from liquid resins in the sets

Remedies.Name: Return the product for a full refund or replacement

recalls.metaDescription: MGA Entertainment recalls Miniverse Make It Mini Sets due to potential skin, eye, and respiratory hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description:
Thousandshores is recalling Theefun Kids Gardening Tools sets, model number HJL-001, due to safety concerns. These sets include five garden tools with wooden handles and brown strings, measuring approximately 27 inches long. The brown handle strings have been found to contain phthalates exceeding federal safety limits, posing health risks to young children if ingested.
Consumers are urged to stop using these gardening tools immediately and ensure they are kept away from young children. Thousandshores is facilitating a full refund process and advises consumers to cut off the brown strings and dispose of them responsibly according to local recycling regulations.
recalls.Title: Thousandshores Recalls Theefun Kids Gardening Tools Due to Phthalate Safety Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Thousandshores via email at support@theefun.com or visit their customer service page at https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=A3G27BXXOHYJZ1.

Images.Caption: Recalled Theefun Kids Gardening Tools, model number HJL-001

Manufacturers.Name: JiuDing Tools Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon from January 2024 to April 2024, priced between $27 and $29.

Hazards.Name: Excessive phthalate levels in brown handle strings pose ingestion risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should remove the tools from children, contact Thousandshores for a refund, and dispose of the strings safely.

recalls.metaDescription: Thousandshores recalls Theefun Kids Gardening Tools due to unsafe phthalate levels. Immediate action required for refunds and safety.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for multiple dress-up playsets from Litti City and Litti Pritti, as well as Prextex slime eggs, due to violations of federal safety standards regarding phthalates and lead content. The Litti City premier doctor playset includes a white outfit with accessories like blue glasses and a stethoscope, while the teacher purse playset features a green purse along with various educational items. The Litti Pritti handbag playset comes with a pink purse and makeup items. The Prextex slime eggs, which are packaged in a blue container, contain colorful slime and additional mini accessories.
recalls.Title: Recall of Litti City and Litti Pritti Dress-up Playsets and Prextex Slime Eggs Due to Hazardous Phthalates and Lead Levels
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Perch at 888-359-4676 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email playsets@realtimeresults.net. Additional details are available online at www.recallrtr.com/playsets, https://www.perchhq.com/ (click on 'Recall'), or https://www.prextex.com/ (click on 'Recall').

Images.Caption: Recalled Litti City Premier Doctor Playset
Recalled Litti Pritti Handbag Playset
Recalled Litti City Teacher Purse Set
Recalled Prextex DIY Take-Along Slime Eggs

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com; the Litti Pritti handbag playset was also sold at Ocean State Job Lot stores in the northeast from July 2021 through April 2023, priced between $9 and $45.

Importers.Name: Whele LLC d/b/a Perch, of Boston, Massachusetts
Amusty Inc. of Staten Island, New York
Bingo Deals of Harriman, New York

Hazards.Name: Excessive phthalate levels and lead content in playsets and slime eggs pose health risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled items and contact Perch for disposal instructions and a full refund after proof of destruction.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert for Litti City and Litti Pritti playsets and Prextex slime eggs due to harmful phthalates and lead levels. Immediate action required.
